title: Channing Desk Hutch
summary: A wide open hutch that sits on top of a desk, divided into cubbies by two adjustable-position dividers and closed in with a plywood back panel.
description:
This is an open shelving unit meant to sit on a desktop, not a standalone cabinet — a single top, a full-width bottom shelf, and two full-height sides, with two dividers cut to fit that split the space into cubbies. **Cut the dividers to fit your own assembled case** rather than trusting a fixed number, since the actual inside height varies slightly with board thickness.

Three 1x3 rails run across the front at cubby height, tying the dividers together and keeping the whole hutch from racking sideways when it's loaded with books or supplies — this front bracing is what lets the piece stay open on three sides without a back panel doing all the structural work.

The plywood back panel closes the whole hutch in and gives it a finished look from behind, and small blocking pieces at the bottom help square up the case before the back goes on. Size the overall footprint to sit centered on whatever desk it's paired with.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit your assembled case.

steps.title: Build the outer case
steps.body: Assemble the top, bottom shelf, and two sides into a full-width box.

steps.title: Fit and add the dividers
steps.body: Measure the assembled case and cut the two dividers to fit, then attach at even spacing.

steps.title: Add the front rails
steps.body: Attach the three front rails across the cubby openings to tie the dividers together and brace the case.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Nail the plywood back panel across the full case, using the bottom blocking to help square it up first.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Channing desk hutch with open cubbies
